title: Nunchaku brings faster 4-bit image generation into Diffusers
friendlyTitle: Large image models can now run with less GPU memory
summary: Hugging Face has integrated Nunchaku-style 4-bit weight-and-activation inference into Diffusers, allowing compatible quantized diffusion checkpoints to load through the familiar from_pretrained workflow. The approach cuts memory use and can speed up generation rather than merely shrinking model weights.
friendlySummary: Hugging Face added an easier way to use 4-bit image models in Diffusers. This can lower memory use and make image generation faster.
whyItMatters: Developers can run capable image models on more attainable hardware with less custom setup. Hardware support varies — newer NVIDIA cards use NVFP4 while earlier generations need INT4 variants — so deployment choices still matter.
friendlyWhyItMatters: More people may be able to run image models on their own computers, but they must choose the version that works with their graphics card.
